title: fix(deps): update github.com/openshift/imagebuilder digest to 86828bf
body:

comments.body:
### ⚠ Artifact update problem

Renovate failed to update an artifact related to this branch. You probably do not want to merge this PR as-is.

♻ Renovate will retry this branch, including artifacts, only when one of the following happens:

 - any of the package files in this branch needs updating, or 
 - the branch becomes conflicted, or
 - you click the rebase/retry checkbox if found above, or
 - you rename this PR's title to start with "rebase!" to trigger it manually

The artifact failure details are included below:

##### File name: go.sum


[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **NOT APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *renovate[bot]* **Once this PR has been reviewed and has the lgtm label**, please assign ashley-cui for approval. For more information see [the Kubernetes Code Review Process](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process). The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fbuildah).
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - **[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/main/OWNERS)** Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

@cevich PTAL
Not sure what this is doing?

> Not sure what this is doing?

Renovate is attempting to update a dependency and automatically runs `go mod tidy`.  That failed because of a dependency problem:

```
...cut...
	github.com/kr/pretty loaded from github.com/kr/pretty@v0.2.1,
	but go 1.16 would select v0.3.0
```

Is there another renovate PR open for `github.com/kr/pretty` maybe?
...oof, no, a similar artifacts error is on all the Renovate PRs by the looks of it.  Maybe this indicates an existing problem in `go.mod`.  @vrothberg or @mtrmac could you help take a peek?

This is a “situation that can happen”, it is not indicative of anyone doing anything wrong.

For the record:

Typically I’d recommend the first action, it is safe and helps:

- Check out the Renovate-created branch
- Run the command
> `go mod tidy -go=1.16 && go mod tidy -go=1.17`
- Run `make vendor` or whatever the project uses.
- Amend the update commit with the result
- Push back into the Renovate-created branch.

Alternatively, some projects have decided on the second approach, and in that case just a `make vendor` without the extra `mod tidy` two-step is sufficient. But the worst thing that can happen with the two-step is that some dependency will be a bit newer, which is not bad.
Thanks @mtrmac and it's true if it picks up some extra deps., that's fine.  Any open PRs for those updated deps will automatically be closed by Renovate.

Updated using the first method.

This adds explicit minimum versions for `github.com/kr/pretty` and `github.com/rogpeppe/go-internal`; both are only dependencies of _tests_ of our dependencies, so this does not actually make any difference on the vendored code.
Good news.  Since those deps are on all the other Renovate PRs also, merging this one should fix the issue for all the others.

> merging this one should fix the issue for all the others.

It seems to have done so — but tests are unhappy. One part of that seems to be strict commit subject line limits. Another might be the need to run `make vendor-in-container` (as opposed to just `make vendor` which I did).
The subject-too-long thing I can work on.  That's a github-action workflow I can likely grok.
